source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The figure shows standing wave pattern on a string of linear mass density 4.0 \times 1n-{-2} kg/ m under a tension of 100 N. The length of the string is 2.0m. Obtain the frequency.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The figure shows standing wave pattern'
the needed harmonic/mode information is not fully recoverable from text alone and must come from the diagram
multi_step: requires extracting the standing-wave mode from the image
then computing wave speed from tension and linear mass density
then relating string length to wavelength and using f = v/lambda to obtain the final frequency
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric target ('37.5 Hz')
physics formula chain is standard and the result can be checked if the mode in the figure is identified
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support basic evaluability
question_text contains a malformed mass-density expression, so verification is somewhat less than perfect

decision_reasons: Question, image, and answer are all present, so the sample retains core semantic support and should stay in the candidate pool.
The problem is highly image-dependent (image_dependency_score 0.96), but the only available image is small (218x130), which may hinder reliable recovery of the standing-wave mode.
The question text appears to contain corrupted numeric notation, which weakens clean verification even though the answer is present.
Because the sample is still likely recoverable but has asset-quality risks, low_priority is safer than reject.
